name: Hare, Rabbit, Veal or Chicken Stew with Herbs & Barley
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 kilograms hare or rabbit joints (or stewing veal or chicken)
15 fresh sage, roughly chopped (or 1 tablespoon dried sage)
recipeInstructions:
In 7th century England, herbs were one of the few flavorings available to cooks...
Melt the butter in a heavy pan. Fry the meat with the leeks and garlic until the vegetables are slightly softened and the meat is lightly browned.
Add the barley, water, vinegar, bay leaves, and seasoning. Bring the mixture to a boil, cover it, and simmer gently for 1 - 1 1/2 hours or until the meat is very tender and falls off the bone. Add the sage and continue cooking for several minutes. Adjust the seasoning to taste and serve in bowls— the barley will act as a vegetable.
